judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with nearly identical substance; Report 2 adds a bit more rigor (notes lack of resume/offset flag and results.jsonl overwrite risk) but this doesn't change coverage of the stated expectations — the two are comparable.

report_1.evidence: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 avg tokens per scoring call ≈ 9,360,000 tokens (~9.4M)... northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000-token window... The full run needs roughly 14–15× more than what's left

report_2.evidence: 5,200 transcripts × 1,800 average tokens per scoring call = ≈ 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M) for the full run... Northpeak (primary, default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000-token window. The run needs ~14.6× what's left — a shortfall of ~8.72M tokens.

report_1.evidence: ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining of a 12,000,000-token window, resetting at 09:10Z. The ~9.36M-token run fits with ~1.4M tokens of headroom... I'd route the whole run to the secondary backend

report_2.evidence: Ridgeline (secondary): 10,800,000 tokens remaining of a 12,000,000-token window — the full ~9.36M-token run fits with ~1.44M tokens (~15%) of headroom... the command I would actually run tonight is: ... --provider secondary

report_1.evidence: I would not run the default path. Launching run_nightly.sh as-is would exhaust northpeak's remaining 640k tokens partway through (~355 of 5,200 transcripts) and then stall or fail on rate limits

report_2.evidence: I would not run run_nightly.sh as-is, because it silently uses the primary/northpeak backend, which has only ~640k tokens left tonight. Launching the ~9.36M-token job there would exhaust the allowance roughly 7% of the way through

report_1.evidence: the usage snapshot is ~3 hours stale (19:25Z vs. 22:30Z now), so before launching I'd refresh the telemetry (or at minimum confirm ridgeline's remaining balance) rather than trust an old number for a 9.4M-token spend

report_2.evidence: Refresh the usage snapshot first if possible. The snapshot is from 19:25Z, ~3 hours stale at the assumed 22:30Z launch; I'd re-capture it (or check the vendor dashboards) to confirm ridgeline still has ≥ ~9.4M tokens before committing
